items.headline: U.S. Futures Mixed Amid Geopolitical Tensions and Continued AI Focus
items.summary: U.S. stock futures are showing mixed movements this Friday morning, with S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 futures slightly down, while Dow Jones futures edge higher. Investors are carefully watching persistent geopolitical tensions between the U.S. and Iran, which continue to influence oil prices, alongside an ongoing focus on the artificial intelligence sector and upcoming earnings reports. Yesterday's session saw major indices close higher, driven by a strong rally in semiconductor stocks. Not financial advice.

items.statContext: Industrial average futures show modest gains.

items.headline: WD-40 Surges on Strong Q3 Results
items.summary: Shares of WD-40 ($WDFC) climbed 14% after the company reported better-than-expected FQ3 results and issued an upbeat FY2026 outlook. Adjusted EPS and revenue surpassed consensus estimates, with revenue up 24% year-over-year.

items.headline: Vodafone Jumps on Stake Sale Agreement
items.summary: Vodafone ($VOD) shares rose 10% after UAE telecom operator e& Group agreed to sell its 16.21% stake in the company for $5.95 billion to a firm controlled by Xavier Niel's family group.

items.headline: PMGC Holdings Up on Merger News
items.summary: PMGC Holdings Inc. ($ELAB) saw a significant jump of 17.94% in premarket trading after disclosing a merger between two of its wholly-owned subsidiaries.

items.headline: Fermi Tumbles After Upsized Note Offering
items.summary: Shares of Fermi ($FRMI) fell 22% after the company priced an upsized $375 million offering of 5.00% convertible senior notes due 2031, with an initial conversion price representing a 30% premium to its previous closing price.

items.headline: Geopolitical Tensions Continue to Weigh on Markets
items.summary: Military clashes between the U.S. and Iran continue to create uncertainty, leading to mixed stock futures and volatile oil prices. However, reports of potential diplomatic talks to resume have offered some calming sentiment.

items.headline: AI Dominates Sector Focus; Chip Stocks Rally, Then Slip
items.summary: Semiconductor stocks demonstrated strong performance yesterday, driving gains across major indices. Information Technology and Communication Services are noted as leading sectors in growth. However, AI-related stocks in Europe saw a slip in early trade today as investors anticipate SK Hynix's Nasdaq debut as a test of AI sentiment.

items.headline: Fed Expected to Hold Rates Steady; Inflation Concerns Persist
items.summary: The CME Group's FedWatch tool indicates a 79.1% likelihood that the Federal Reserve will maintain current interest rates at its July meeting. Inflation worries remain a key concern for investors, influenced by fluctuating oil prices and their potential impact on consumer costs.

items.headline: BitMine Reports Earnings Today; ChipMOS Sees AI-Driven Revenue Boost
items.summary: BitMine ($BMNG) is scheduled to release its earnings report today, July 10, 2026. Separately, ChipMOS Technologies Inc. ($IMOS) reported record high monthly and quarterly revenue for June and Q2 2026, citing persistent AI-related demand as a key driver.
